title: Bind raw request to declared intent and repository capability
outOfScope: Broad multilingual and hidden-risk phrase coverage reserved for Work Item 3.
Release publication and execution-plan cleanup reserved for Work Items 7 and 8.

sources.reason: Comprehensive review identifies scope-path inference as insufficient capability evidence.
Current capability guard infers requirements from scope paths.
Existing capability and intent guard regression coverage.
Preflight assembles Trust Layer signals and determines readiness.

riskAssessment.reason: Bounded guard change with explicit raw-request evidence and regression coverage.

executionDecision.reason: User authorized automatic serial remediation and the guard boundary is explicit.

preReviewWarnings: Review raw-request binding and fail-closed unsupported capability handling.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: A raw user request is explicit evidence and cannot be replaced by scope-path interpretation.
Declared intent must be checked against repository capabilities before implementation continues.
Unsupported physical or external operations are fail-closed even when the requested scope contains documentation or tests.
Regression tests and Trust Layer documentation explain the boundary and reserve broad multilingual coverage for Work Item 3.
guidelines: Keep raw-request binding deterministic and repository/project neutral.

intent.problem: The current Capability Guard derives required capabilities from changed path names, allowing an unsafe raw request to be reframed as documentation or test work.
constraints: Raw user request evidence must remain persisted in the Work Item Contract.
Unsupported physical and external-operation boundaries must fail closed.
Do not attempt broad multilingual or hidden-risk interpretation in this Work Item.
intent.rationale: Checking raw-request boundary evidence before scope-derived capability inference prevents a request such as making a rocket from being made safe merely by choosing docs or tests as the declared scope.

rawUserRequest: Bind the raw request to declared intent and repository capability; do not reinterpret unsupported real-world operations as documentation or tests.

declaredIntent.summary: Make Trust Layer capability readiness reflect the user's request and the repository's declared boundaries.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: A supported governance request is accepted when raw request, intent, and capabilities align.
An unsupported rocket-building request is blocked despite a docs or tests scope.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized automatic serial execution, governance changes, PR merge, and branch cleanup.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
